response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and web developer with a strong TypeScript/Node.js background, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The job requires native-level Japanese proficiency, which is entirely absent from the resume. Beyond the language blocker, the role demands deep embedded C development for LAN-switch firmware, Linux kernel investigation and modification, and hands-on knowledge of network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), none of which appear in his work history. His C/C++ exposure is limited to a bachelor thesis project and early IDE tooling work, far below the embedded systems depth required.
strengths: 25+ years of software development experience across a wide range of domains and programming languages
Some exposure to C/C++ and low-latency systems work (NI LabWindows, DLang async I/O at Sociomantic)
Experience with Linux and embedded contexts mentioned at Altom (robotic arms, hardware control)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a non-negotiable hard requirement for this position
No experience with network security protocols, Layer-2/3 switching, or embedded networking firmware
No Linux kernel development or source-code modification experience; Linux listed only as a general skill
